choice: lib/insight/* (pure, 8 tests) + queries.ts (DB). Attribution (attribution.ts): diff the two most recent position batches; per holding ΔMarketValue = oldQty·(newPrice−oldPrice) [price effect] + (newQty−oldQty)·newPrice [flow effect], summing to ΔMV; opened/closed positions fall back to the available price. Allocation (allocation.ts): classifyHolding via a built-in symbol→AssetClass map (us_equity/intl_equity/bonds/cash/real_estate, unknown→other); computeAllocation aggregates latest-batch holdings per class with current% and drift vs the household.targetAllocation jsonb (fractions). Rebalance: rebalanceSuggestions = target·total − currentValue per class, dropping sub- noise, netting to ~0; no optimization or tax-lot logic (v1). UI: attribution 'what moved my number' on Assets, allocation+drift+rebalance on a new /allocation screen (added to nav), target editor persists percentages as fractions. Positions seeded by E*TRADE sync (E6-S3).
rationale: [build-originated] 99d5cbfb listed attribution/allocation/rebalance as v1 surfaces but not the math: the price-vs-flow decomposition, the symbol→asset-class map, target stored as household jsonb fractions, and drift-to-target rebalancing that nets to zero with a noise threshold. Pure modules keep it unit-testable independent of live position data.
